mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean during rate hikes with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 55% of instances when unemployment rises > 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real short-term interest rates and declines in housing starts indicate tightening financial conditions and weakening residential demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict negative returns with Rank IC > 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening economic output and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with industrial production growth (t-stat < 1.5).

mechanism_narrative: Declining consumer price inflation reduces expectations for aggressive monetary tightening, lowering discount rates and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive forward returns do not materialize during disinflationary periods with statistical significance (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market expansion, supporting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal yields negative or flat forward returns in > 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0 percent signals strong aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth surpasses 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth during periods of elevated inflation signals supply constraints and potential central bank tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive during supply-constrained inflation episodes with p < 0.05.
